tools.name: pprof (google/pprof)
tools.summary: Tool for visualization and analysis of profiling data in the profile.proto format. Reads CPU/heap/mutex/block profiles and produces text…
tools.when_to_use: When you have a Go service (or any pprof-format profile) and need to find CPU hotspots, memory allocation sources, lock contention, or goroutine blocking. Use…
tools.usage: Install: `go install github.com/google/pprof@latest`. Capture (Go): hit `/debug/pprof/profile?seconds=30` or `import _ "net/http/pprof"`. Analyze: `pprof -http=:8080 ./binary profile.pb.gz` (web UI) or `pprof -top ./binary profile.pb.gz` / `pprof -web cpu.pprof`.

tools.name: Valgrind Callgrind
tools.summary: Callgrind is a Valgrind tool that records the call graph and per-function/per-line instruction counts of a program via dynamic binary…
tools.when_to_use: When you need exact, repeatable instruction-level profiling of a native (C/C++/Rust/etc.) binary to find the precise call path and source lines that dominate…
tools.install: apt install valgrind / brew install valgrind (Linux primaril
tools.usage: Install: `apt install valgrind` / `brew install valgrind` (Linux primarily). Run: `valgrind --tool=callgrind ./mybinary args`, then `callgrind_annotate callgrind.out.<pid>` for text, or open in `kcachegrind`. Add `--cache-sim=yes --branch-sim=yes` for cache/branch modeling.

tools.name: Linux perf (perf_events)
tools.summary: The standard Linux system profiler, part of the kernel source tree. Uses hardware performance counters, tracepoints, kprobes/uprobes and…
tools.when_to_use: When profiling any native or JIT workload on Linux and you need low-overhead, whole-system CPU hotspot analysis, cache-miss/branch-miss counts, or…
tools.usage: Install: `apt install linux-tools-common linux-tools-$(uname -r)` (Linux only). Counters: `perf stat ./app`. Sample + report: `perf record -g -- ./app args && perf report`. Live: `perf top`. Flame graph: `perf record -F 99 -ag -- sleep 30` then fold with FlameGraph scripts.

tools.name: Memray
tools.summary: Memory profiler for Python by Bloomberg that tracks every allocation in Python code, native extension modules, and the interpreter itself.…
tools.when_to_use: When a Python program (or its C/C++ extensions) leaks memory, has unexpected peak RSS, or allocates excessively under load. Use to find the exact allocation…
tools.usage: Install: `python3 -m pip install memray`. Record: `python3 -m memray run -o out.bin myscript.py`. Report: `memray flamegraph out.bin` (or `memray table out.bin`, `memray tree out.bin`). In tests: `pip install pytest-memray` then `pytest --memray`.

tools.name: Scalene
tools.summary: High-precision CPU, GPU, and memory profiler for Python with very low overhead. Distinguishes time spent in Python vs native code vs…
tools.when_to_use: When you need a single tool to pinpoint both CPU and memory hotspots in a Python program at line granularity and to tell whether time is going into Python…
tools.usage: Install: `python3 -m pip install -U scalene`. Profile: `scalene your_prog.py` (opens web UI), or terminal-only: `scalene --cli your_prog.py`, or reduced scope: `scalene --reduced-profile your_prog.py`. JSON output: `scalene --json --outfile prof.json your_prog.py`.

tools.name: Austin
tools.summary: Frame-stack sampling profiler for CPython written in pure C. Reads the interpreter's virtual memory to sample running thread stacks with…
tools.when_to_use: When you want an extremely lightweight, dependency-free statistical profiler for a Python process (including production or multi-process/mod_wsgi workloads)…
tools.usage: Install: `pip install austin-dist` (or `brew install austin`/`snap install austin`). Profile a script: `austin -o profile.austin python myscript.py`. Attach: `austin -p <pid> -o profile.austin`. Convert/visualize with austin-python or flamegraph tooling, or use `austin-tui`.

tools.name: Tracy Profiler
tools.summary: Real-time, nanosecond-resolution hybrid frame-and-sampling profiler with remote telemetry, aimed at games and latency-critical apps.…
tools.when_to_use: When profiling a frame-based or latency-sensitive C/C++ application (game loop, renderer, audio/realtime pipeline) and per-frame timeline detail, lock…
tools.usage: Install: add the `public/` tree (or `tracy` via vcpkg/Conan) to your build, define `TRACY_ENABLE`, link `TracyClient.cpp`. Instrument with `#include "tracy/Tracy.hpp"` and `ZoneScoped;` / `FrameMark;` macros. Build the server GUI from `profiler/` (or download a release) and connect to the running app to capture/inspect.

tools.name: Airspeed Velocity (asv)
tools.summary: Benchmarking framework for tracking Python package performance across its git history. Runs a suite of timing/memory/peak-memory/custom…
tools.when_to_use: When maintaining a Python library and you want continuous performance regression tracking across commits (not just a single run), e.g. to catch which commit…
tools.usage: Install: `pip install asv`. Init: `asv quickstart`, define benchmarks in `benchmarks/`. Run on current env: `asv run`. Compare two commits: `asv continuous main HEAD`. Bisect a regression: `asv find <good>..<bad> <benchmark>`. Publish dashboard: `asv publish && asv preview`.

tools.name: pyperf
tools.summary: PSF-maintained toolkit to write, run, and analyze Python microbenchmarks reliably. Auto-calibrates iteration counts for a time budget,…
tools.when_to_use: When you need statistically trustworthy Python microbenchmarks and want to minimize measurement noise (it powers the official CPython pyperformance suite).…
tools.usage: Install: `python3 -m pip install pyperf`. Quick: `python3 -m pyperf timeit '[1,2]*1000' -o bench.json`. Compare: `python3 -m pyperf compare_to old.json new.json`. Or write a script with `pyperf.Runner().bench_func(...)`. System tuning: `sudo python3 -m pyperf system tune`.

tools.name: bpftrace
tools.summary: High-level tracing language and tool for Linux built on eBPF. Lets you write awk/C-like one-liners and scripts that attach to kprobes,…
tools.when_to_use: When diagnosing performance or behavior of a running Linux system/process under load and you need custom, ad-hoc dynamic instrumentation: tail-latency…
tools.install: apt install bpftrace / dnf install bpftrace (Linux
tools.usage: Install: `apt install bpftrace` / `dnf install bpftrace` (Linux, needs root/CAP_BPF). One-liner example (syscall counts by process): `bpftrace -e 'tracepoint:raw_syscalls:sys_enter { @[comm] = count(); }'`. Profile on-CPU stacks: `bpftrace -e 'profile:hz:99 /pid==$1/ { @[ustack] = count(); }' <pid>`. Run a script: `bpftrace script.bt`.

tools.name: EvoSuite
tools.summary: Automatically generates JUnit test suites for Java classes using an evolutionary/genetic-algorithm search that maximizes code coverage…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when a Java/JVM project (Maven/Gradle) has classes with little or no unit-test coverage and you want to auto-generate a regression suite and surface…
tools.usage: Download the jar: `wget https://github.com/EvoSuite/evosuite/releases/download/v1.2.0/evosuite-1.2.0.jar`. Run: `java -jar evosuite-1.2.0.jar -class com.example.MyClass -projectCP target/classes`. Generated tests land in `evosuite-tests/`. Maven plugin also available.

tools.name: Randoop
tools.summary: Feedback-directed random test generation for Java. Builds sequences of method/constructor calls, executes them, and uses the results to…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger on a Java project where you want fast, no-config generation of JUnit tests that expose API contract violations and crash-inducing call sequences.…
tools.usage: Download `randoop-all-<ver>.jar` from the GitHub releases, then: `java -classpath target/classes:randoop-all.jar randoop.main.Main gentests --testclass=com.example.MyClass --time-limit=60`. Emits `RegressionTest*.java` and `ErrorTest*.java`.

tools.name: Pynguin
tools.summary: Automated unit-test generation for Python. Uses search-based (evolutionary) and random algorithms to generate pytest/unittest-style test…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger on a typed-ish Python module/package lacking unit tests, when you want auto-generated pytest cases and to flush out crashing inputs / unhandled…
tools.usage: `pip install pynguin`, then `pynguin --project-path ./src --output-path ./tests --module-name mypkg.mymodule` (set `PYNGUIN_DANGER_AWARE=1`). Requires Python >=3.10 (experimental up to 3.14). Run inside Docker for safety.

tools.name: CrossHair
tools.summary: Concolic / symbolic-execution analysis for Python. Repeatedly calls functions with symbolic inputs backed by the Z3 SMT solver to explore…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when Python functions have explicit contracts (asserts, type hints, icontract/deal decorators) and you want to prove/refute them by finding minimal…
tools.usage: `pip install crosshair-tool`, then `crosshair check path/to/module.py` (one-shot), `crosshair watch path/to/pkg/` (continuous), or `crosshair cover module.func` to emit counterexample-driven tests.

tools.name: Hypothesis
tools.summary: Property-based testing library for Python. Generates and shrinks randomized inputs against user-declared properties (including metamorphic…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when you want to write or auto-derive property/metamorphic tests for Python functions (encode/decode round-trips, invariants, idempotence). Best…
tools.usage: `pip install hypothesis`, then use `from hypothesis import given, strategies as st` and decorate pytest tests. Auto-scaffold: `hypothesis write mypkg.myfunc > test_myfunc.py`, run with `pytest`.

tools.name: fast-check
tools.summary: Property-based testing framework for JavaScript/TypeScript (QuickCheck-style). Generates randomized inputs with automatic shrinking to…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger on a JS/TS codebase (works with Jest/Vitest/Mocha) where you want to fuzz pure functions and find edge-case bugs - off-by-one, unicode, NaN,…
tools.usage: `npm install -D fast-check`, then `import fc from 'fast-check'` and wrap assertions in `fc.assert(fc.property(fc.string(), s => ...))`. Run via your existing test runner (`npx vitest` / `npx jest`).

tools.name: Schemathesis
tools.summary: Property-based API testing tool (built on Hypothesis) that reads an OpenAPI or GraphQL schema, generates thousands of conforming and…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when the target project exposes a REST/GraphQL API with an OpenAPI/GraphQL schema and you want to auto-fuzz endpoints to surface 500s,…
tools.usage: `pip install schemathesis` (or `uvx schemathesis`), then `schemathesis run http://localhost:8000/openapi.json` against a running service. Can also generate pytest cases via the `schemathesis` Python API.

tools.name: EvoMaster
tools.summary: AI/evolutionary system-level test (fuzz) generator for web APIs. Black-box mode works from an OpenAPI/GraphQL/RPC schema; white-box mode…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger on a REST/GraphQL/gRPC service (especially JVM/.NET/Node for white-box) where you want coverage-driven fuzzing that finds 500 errors and faults and…
tools.usage: `pip install evomaster` (v6+) then `evomaster --blackBoxMode true --bbSwagger http://localhost:8080/v3/api-docs --outputFormat JAVA_JUNIT_5`. White-box mode needs the language-specific driver/instrumentation.

tools.name: KLEE
tools.summary: Symbolic execution engine built on LLVM. Runs a program on symbolic inputs, explores feasible paths via an SMT solver, and automatically…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger on C/C++ (or any LLVM-bitcode) code, especially parsers and utility libraries, when you want high-coverage test inputs and to find…
tools.usage: Easiest via Docker: `docker pull klee/klee`. Compile target: `clang -emit-llvm -c -g -O0 prog.c -o prog.bc`, then `klee --libc=uclibc --posix-runtime prog.bc`. Replay tests with `klee-replay` / libkleeRuntest.

tools.name: angr
tools.summary: Python binary-analysis platform with a symbolic/concolic execution engine (claripy + Z3). Loads compiled binaries, explores paths…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when you only have a compiled binary (no source) or want to drive execution to a specific dangerous state (e.g. a buffer overflow or auth-bypass…
tools.usage: `pip install angr`, then in Python: `import angr; p = angr.Project('./bin'); s = p.factory.entry_state(); sm = p.factory.simulation_manager(s); sm.explore(find=0xADDR)` then `sm.found[0].posix.dumps(0)` for the triggering input.

tools.name: Atheris
tools.summary: Coverage-guided native fuzzing engine for Python (and CPython native extensions), built on libFuzzer. Instruments bytecode for coverage…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when a Python project parses untrusted/complex input (binary formats, deserialization, decoders) or wraps a C extension, and you want coverage-guided…
tools.usage: `pip install atheris`, write a harness with `atheris.Setup(sys.argv, TestOneInput)` + `atheris.Fuzz()`, then run `python fuzz_harness.py`. For native code build with `atheris.instrument_all()` / sanitizers.

tools.name: Manticore
tools.summary: Symbolic execution tool (Trail of Bits) for binaries (x86/ARM), Linux ELF, WASM, and EVM smart contracts. Explores program states with…
tools.when_to_use: Trigger when analyzing a binary or an Ethereum/EVM smart contract and you want to symbolically verify properties or generate inputs reaching a failure state.…
tools.usage: `pip install manticore`. CLI: `manticore ./binary` (explores and dumps testcases to `mcore_*/`). Smart contracts: `manticore contract.sol`. Or use the Python API `from manticore.native import Manticore`.
